parts.title: Chemistry Teachers
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a chemistry lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. chemical reactions], with three sections focusing on theory, experiment, and analysis, and a link to the national curriculum.
Give me five strategies for teaching grade [X] students about [specific theme, e.g. elements], with a practical exercise for each and a reflection on its value.
Write a short guide for introducing [specific theme, e.g. acids and bases] to a grade [X] class, with three steps and an engaging demonstration activity.
Suggest three ways to use digital tools to teach [specific theme, e.g. the periodic table] in grade [X], with a simulation for each and a link to digital competence in the national curriculum.
Create a list of five experimental tasks for a grade [X] class on [specific theme, e.g. mixtures], with a hypothesis and an analysis question for each.
Give me an example of a routine for starting a chemistry lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. atoms], with three steps and a connection to everyday phenomena.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. chemical bonds] into a grade [X] class with practical exercises, with an experiment for each and a reflection.
Create an assessment rubric for a chemistry lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. reactions], with three knowledge requirements from the national curriculum and criteria for different levels.
Give me five ideas for teaching [specific theme, e.g. acids and bases] in grade [X], with a creative activity for each and a link to chemical concepts.
Write a short template for planning a chemistry lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. organic chemistry], with three sections (introduction, experiment, reflection) and a problem-solving task.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with a biology teacher on a cross-curricular project about [specific theme, e.g. chemical processes in nature] in grade [X], with one idea for each and a goal.
Give me an example of a lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. mixtures], with three steps focusing on experiments and an analysis task.
Create a list of five discussion questions for a grade [X] class on [specific theme, e.g. chemical reactions], with a focus on sustainability and a link to current issues.
Suggest three ways to use everyday examples to teach [specific theme, e.g. acids and bases] in grade [X], with a demonstration for each and an analysis of the phenomenon.
Write a short guide for teaching [specific theme, e.g. the periodic table] in grade [X], with three steps and a link to scientific inquiry methods in the national curriculum.
Give me five ideas for making [specific theme, e.g. chemical bonds] more engaging for a grade [X] class, with a practical activity for each and a rationale.
Suggest three ways to assess students' understanding of [specific theme, e.g. reactions] in grade [X], with a method for each and a link to the national curriculum's knowledge requirements.
Create a plan for a chemistry lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. atoms], with three sections and a closing reflection task on chemical processes.
Give me an example of an experimental exercise for a grade [X] class on [specific theme, e.g. organic chemistry], with three steps and an analysis of results.
Write a list of three short ways to connect [specific theme, e.g. mixtures] to students' everyday lives in a grade [X] class, with a question for each and a link to the national curriculum.
Create a detailed plan for a project on [specific theme, e.g. chemical reactions] for a grade [X] class, with three lessons focusing on experiments and sustainability solutions.
Give me five strategies for teaching [specific theme, e.g. the periodic table] in grade [X], with an experimental activity for each and a link to chemical concepts.
Write a short guide for using simulations in a chemistry lesson on [specific theme, e.g. acids and bases] in grade [X], with three steps and an analysis task.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. chemical bonds] with physics in a grade [X] class, with a cross-curricular activity for each and a goal.
Create a list of five creative problem-solving tasks for a grade [X] class on [specific theme, e.g. mixtures], with instructions for each and a connection to everyday phenomena.
Give me an example of a routine for closing a chemistry lesson on [specific theme, e.g. reactions] in grade [X], with three steps and a reflection question.
Suggest three ways to use experiments to teach [specific theme, e.g. organic chemistry] in grade [X], with a lab activity for each and an analysis of results.
